description: Deferred operations queue. Items land here when resources are unavailable or operations need human approval before execution.

schema.blocked_reason: Why this item is blocked (if status=blocked)

queue.description: CRITICAL: Investigate and fix console/Claude Code mobile input issues — system is inserting predicted words/sentences while typing, making terminal and Claude Code unusable on mobile devices

symptoms: After typing first few words, system removes or adds predicted words/sentences
Affects both raw terminal and Claude Code services
CONFIRMED: Disabling mobile predictive text did NOT fix the issue — problem is NOT the keyboard
ruled_out: Mobile keyboard predictive text / autocorrect (disabled, issue persists)
investigation_steps: Check if this is a terminal emulator issue (which app? Termux, JuiceSSH, etc.)
Check if SSH client is sending autocomplete/predictive signals
Test with different terminal apps to isolate
Check if Claude Code CLI has readline/input handling issues on mobile TTY
Investigate terminal echo/line editing mode (stty -echo, raw mode)
Check stty/terminfo settings for the mobile session
Check if bash readline or Claude Code ink/react-ink is mishandling mobile TTY dimensions or resize events
Test with a basic SSH session (no Claude Code) to isolate whether issue is terminal-level or app-level

queue.description: Fix Grok idea generator — not outputting anything, only echoing back input JSON

symptoms: Grok idea generator returns only the input JSON with no new ideas generated
No output from the model, just input passthrough
investigation_steps: Check Grok API call in idea generator — verify model response is being parsed correctly
Check if response format changed (Grok API update?)
Verify API key and endpoint are correct
Check if streaming vs non-streaming response handling is mismatched
Log raw Grok API response to identify what's actually being returned

queue.description: R&D: Research Anthropic Agent SDK as alternative to claude -p for Telegram bridge — could improve latency, enable streaming responses, reduce subprocess overhead

payload.source_title: I Replaced OpenClaw With Claude Code in One Day — Mark Kashef
research_topics: Anthropic Agent SDK (@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk) — capabilities, API surface, subprocess model
Compare Agent SDK subprocess vs current claude -p fire-and-forget approach
Persistent session management — does Agent SDK maintain state across calls?
Streaming response support — can we pipe Agent SDK output to Telegram in real-time?
Resource overhead — Agent SDK daemon vs spawning claude -p per request
Compatibility with existing OPAI skills, MCP servers, CLAUDE.md context
Terms of service implications — verify Agent SDK usage is compliant with Claude Max plan

queue.description: R&D: Add multimodal support to Telegram bridge — photo/video/voice interpretation via Claude Code vision pipeline

payload.source_title: I Replaced OpenClaw With Claude Code in One Day — Mark Kashef
research_topics: SCC IDE vision pipeline pattern (stream-json envelope) — can it be adapted for Telegram?
grammY file handling — download photo/video from Telegram API to temp file
Claude Code --input-format stream-json with image content blocks
Voice note handling — transcription before Claude processing vs direct audio
File size limits and compression for Telegram media
User experience — inline response vs 'processing...' indicator for heavy media

queue.description: R&D: Build GEO audit agent squad for HELM — 5-dimension scoring methodology, website crawling, PDF report generation, agency service delivery pipeline

payload.source_title: Claude Code Just KILLED All Marketing Agencies — Zubair Trabzada
research_topics: 5-dimension GEO scoring methodology (AI Citability, Brand Authority, Technical GEO, Content Quality, Schema)
Website crawling approach — Playwright headless vs curl-based fetching for audit data collection
llms.txt spec — format, compliance checking, generation
robots.txt AI crawler detection (GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended)
Schema markup detection and scoring (Organization, FAQ, Product, Person, Offer, Review)
Per-AI-platform visibility testing — can we query each platform programmatically?
PDF report generation for OPAI — WeasyPrint vs Puppeteer vs reportlab vs HTML-to-PDF
Integration with HELM business management pipeline — audit > outreach > deliver > invoice

queue.description: R&D: PDF report generation capability for OPAI — upgrade agent reports, HITL briefings, HELM deliverables from markdown to polished PDF

payload.source_title: Claude Code Just KILLED All Marketing Agencies — Zubair Trabzada
research_topics: Python PDF libraries: WeasyPrint (HTML/CSS to PDF), reportlab (programmatic), fpdf2
Node.js options: Puppeteer/Playwright PDF generation from HTML, pdfkit
Template approach — HTML/CSS report template + data injection vs programmatic layout
Branding — OPAI/HELM branded report templates with logos, colors, charts
Chart generation — matplotlib/chart.js for score gauges, bar charts, radar charts
Integration points — post_squad_hook.py, HELM report generation, HITL briefings
File storage — where do generated PDFs live? reports/ or served via tool API?

queue.description: R&D: Crypto trading strategy automation — research open-source backtesting engines, Pine Script generation, iterative strategy improvement loops, and scoring methodologies. Build a library reference for later HELM use.

payload.source_title: Claude AI can NOW Automatically Build and Improve Your TradingView Strategies — Michael Automates
research_topics: Open-source Python backtesting frameworks — Backtrader, vectorbt, Zipline, backtesting.py, freqtrade. Feature comparison, active maintenance, ease of integration with AI agents
Pine Script fundamentals — syntax, strategy structure, indicator functions. Enough for Claude to generate and modify TradingView strategies programmatically
OHLCV data sourcing — free APIs for historical crypto data (Binance API, CoinGecko, CCXT library, Yahoo Finance). Data formats, rate limits, caching strategies
Iterative strategy improvement pattern — how to structure an AI loop: generate strategy → backtest → score → mutate → repeat. Convergence criteria, iteration limits, diversity preservation

Multi-asset scoring methodology — testing strategies across multiple assets/timeframes to avoid overfitting. Risk-reward scoring, Sharpe ratio, max drawdown weighting, win rate vs profit factor tradeoffs
Strategy evaluation metrics — P&L%, max drawdown, Sharpe ratio, Sortino ratio, Calmar ratio, win rate, profit factor, number of trades. Which metrics matter most and why
Risk management for automated trading — position sizing, stop-loss strategies, portfolio diversification, correlation analysis between assets
Regulatory and liability considerations — what can HELM offer as a service vs financial advice? Educational content vs managed trading. Disclaimers and compliance boundaries
Open-source alternatives to TradingView — Grafana + data feeds, lightweight-charts (TradingView open-source lib), custom charting for strategy visualization
Integration with OPAI agent framework — how would a trading strategy squad work? Roles: data fetcher, strategy generator, backtester, scorer, reporter. Squad config pattern

queue.description: R&D: AI task offloading via OpenRouter — reduce Claude subscription usage by routing bulk/repetitive/customer-facing tasks to cheaper models. Extend claude_api.py with multi-model fallback. Design HELM business AI layer.

payload.source_title: These FREE APIs Unlock EVERY AI Model — iampauljames
research_topics: OpenRouter integration — sign up, get API key, test free models (Llama 3.3 70B, DeepSeek R1, Qwen3) for OPAI-relevant tasks
Extend tools/shared/claude_api.py — add OpenRouter as fallback tier: Anthropic API → OpenRouter (cheaper models) → Claude CLI. Model selection by task complexity

Task classification for model routing — which OPAI tasks can use free/cheap models vs which need Claude? Build a routing matrix: classification/triage → free, content gen → cheap, code/analysis → mid, reasoning/planning → Claude
HELM business AI layer design — how do HELM-managed businesses get AI capabilities? OpenRouter API key per business? Shared key with usage tracking? Cost allocation model?
Puter.js evaluation — is the User-Pays model viable for HELM web products? Can end users cover their own AI costs? UX friction analysis
BYOK strategy — should OPAI register its own provider keys (Anthropic, OpenAI) through OpenRouter for unified routing + automatic failover? Cost comparison: direct API vs OpenRouter BYOK

Free model benchmarking — test Llama 3.3 70B, DeepSeek R1, Qwen3, Gemma 3 on OPAI-relevant tasks: email classification, content summarization, data extraction, code review. Quality vs Claude comparison
Rate limit management — OpenRouter free tier is 50-1000 req/day. How to design a queue/throttle system for bulk tasks that respects limits?
Security and data handling — OpenRouter zero data retention option, BYOK encryption, privacy implications for client data passing through third-party gateway
Cost projection — model current OPAI Claude usage, estimate what percentage could be offloaded to cheaper models, calculate monthly savings
